rationale: All 7 acceptance criteria pass. Both fix sites apply the identical formula; no logic errors, no security issues, no public API breakage. Two deferred findings are pre-existing patterns not introduced by this diff: (1) ORM objects used outside the async-session block in list_project_budget_members — the original code already did this for allocations; adding budget.max_budget to the same pattern is safe today because all scalar columns are loaded eagerly, but fragile if the query is later changed to defer columns. (2) SettingsService.get_enforce_member_spend_limits is a synchronous DB call inside an async handler — technical analysis confirms this pattern is already established in other routers. Dismissed: dead assignment guard (service raises 404 before it fires), aggregate semantics (N x max_budget total is intentional and documented with a comment). 1 dismissed test gap: test_list_project_budget_members_returns_effective_budget_when_enforcement_disabled does not assert allocated_soft_budget unchanged in the enforcement-disabled path, but AC-7 is covered by the two _build_project_budget_response tests.

business_review.description: GET /members returns budget.max_budget per member and N x budget.max_budget total when enforcement disabled
Both endpoints return persisted allocation.allocated_max_budget when enforcement enabled
_build_project_budget_response applies effective formula to both per-member value and aggregate total
LiteLLM sync unchanged — project_member_runtime_sync.py not touched
Rebalance behaviour unchanged when enforcement enabled
Regression test for enforcement-disabled read path present and correct
allocated_soft_budget not changed by enforcement flag

standards_review.notes: Both commits follow EPMCDME-####: Description format
make ruff passes clean after line-length fix
No auth changes; existing authenticate dependency unchanged; no secrets introduced

deferred: ORM objects (budget, assignment, allocations) used outside async-with session block in list_project_budget_members — pre-existing pattern, safe today, fragile if repository queries add deferred columns
SettingsService.get_enforce_member_spend_limits is a synchronous DB read inside an async handler — pre-existing architectural pattern in this codebase
test_list_project_budget_members_returns_effective_budget_when_enforcement_disabled does not assert allocated_soft_budget unchanged in the enforcement-disabled path
